name: Pyramid Credit Repair
description_short: New York-based credit repair company founded in 2011, offering three-bureau dispute services, dedicated case managers, and flexible no-contract plans starting at $89/month.
description_long:
Pyramid Credit Repair is a New York-based credit repair company that has been helping consumers improve their credit since 2011. Operating under a dedicated case manager model, the company assigns a personal representative to each client who oversees their credit repair journey from start to finish. Pyramid works with all three major credit bureaus — Equifax, Experian, and TransUnion — to identify and challenge inaccurate, outdated, or unverifiable negative items on your credit reports. The company offers a 7-day free trial across all plan tiers, which is notably uncommon in the credit repair industry and reflects a degree of confidence in their service quality.

The company operates three service tiers: Bronze at $89/month, Gold at $129/month, and Platinum at $179/month. The Bronze plan covers the essentials — three-bureau disputes, an online tracking portal, monthly progress reports, and credit score analysis — making it one of the more affordable full-service credit repair options available. Higher tiers add enhanced dispute services, creditor negotiations, and round-the-clock support. Pyramid also offers a couples plan at $198/month and an optional identity theft protection add-on at $29.99/month. There are no long-term contracts; clients can pause, postpone, or cancel at any time without penalty.

What distinguishes Pyramid Credit Repair from many competitors is their combination of flexibility and targeted discounts. The company offers a 15% discount for teachers and a 20% discount for veterans — meaningful gestures toward service members and educators who often face credit challenges. Their A+ BBB rating with only one complaint filed in three years speaks to a professional and responsive operation, even if the company has not pursued formal BBB accreditation. Their affiliate program, the Credit Repair Alliance, is targeted at mortgage brokers, lenders, and real estate professionals and pays recurring monthly commissions as long as referrals remain active clients.

However, Pyramid Credit Repair has notable drawbacks that prospective clients should weigh carefully. The company does not offer a money-back guarantee, which is a meaningful gap compared to larger competitors that provide that safety net. Their setup fee is acknowledged but not publicly disclosed, creating transparency concerns before signing up. The service is also unavailable in approximately 16 states, limiting their reach compared to national alternatives. Customer reviews are positive on Trustpilot (4.6/5) and BestCompany (4.4/5), but considerably lower on TopConsumerReviews (3.0/5), suggesting some inconsistency in the client experience. For consumers who qualify geographically and value flexibility over formal guarantees, Pyramid Credit Repair offers a credible and accessible option.

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee offered. A setup fee is disclosed as existing but the exact amount is not publicly listed on the website. Clients may cancel at any time without contract penalty; however, payments for work already completed may still apply.

pros: 7-day free trial on all plans — uncommon in the credit repair industry
No long-term contracts with flexible pause, postpone, or cancel options
Dedicated case manager assigned to every client on all tiers
A+ BBB rating with only one complaint filed in three years
Teacher (15%) and veteran (20%) discounts reduce ongoing cost
Bronze plan at $89/month is among the more affordable full-service options
Works with all three major credit bureaus simultaneously
cons: No money-back guarantee offered, unlike several major competitors
Setup fee exists but exact amount is not publicly disclosed
Not available in approximately 16 states, limiting geographic reach
Low review volume relative to national competitors reduces confidence in ratings
Mobile app availability is unverified with conflicting information across review sources
best_for: Budget-conscious consumers seeking full three-bureau dispute coverage
Teachers and military veterans who qualify for meaningful discount pricing
Consumers who want flexibility with no long-term contract commitment
Clients who prefer a dedicated case manager throughout the entire process
Individuals who want to test the service risk-free with a 7-day trial
diagnosis: A credible affordable option for consumers in eligible states wanting three-bureau dispute coverage with no long-term commitment. Main caveats: no money-back guarantee and an undisclosed setup fee reduce upfront transparency.
services: Credit report analysis and error identification
Dispute filing with all three major credit bureaus
Dedicated case manager support throughout service
Online client portal for real-time progress tracking
typical_results_timeline: Most clients begin to see initial dispute results within 30-60 days. Meaningful credit score improvements typically occur within 3-6 months, with complex negative item cases potentially requiring 6-12 months of active service.
